title: Reasons for determination — July 3, 2026
summary: The Canadian International Trade Tribunal ruled that Chinese steel fasteners harm domestic producers, so imports will face duties to protect Canadian manufacturers from unfair pricing.

title: Reasons for determination — June 24, 2026
summary: Canadian manufacturers of seamless steel pipe now face a 15.4% anti-dumping duty on imports from China, increasing costs for buyers of this critical industrial input.

title: Determination — June 18, 2026
summary: The Canadian International Trade Tribunal ruled that imported steel pipes don't harm Canadian producers enough to warrant tariffs, so Canadian businesses can continue importing them at regular rates.

title: Determination — June 9, 2026
summary: The Canadian International Trade Tribunal ruled that dumped steel fasteners from China harm Canadian makers, so import duties now apply to protect the domestic industry.

title: Reasons for determination — June 1, 2026
summary: Canadian companies exporting steel products to the U.S. may face new tariff increases starting June 2026, requiring them to reassess pricing and supply chain strategies for American customers.

title: Reasons for finding — May 19, 2026
summary: The Canadian International Trade Tribunal found that imported steel pipes harm Canadian producers, so new tariffs will protect domestic manufacturers from unfair pricing competition.

title: Determination — May 15, 2026
summary: Canada's International Trade Tribunal ruled that Chinese steel pipe imports unfairly undercut domestic prices, triggering new tariffs that will raise costs for Canadian manufacturers using these materials.
